original_image_tags: A single page of light blue/green paper, appearing to be the back or routing slip of a document. The page has two punched holes at the top and is slightly torn at the top right corner. There is faint, reversed red text visible through the paper from the other side. The page is covered with several ink stamps, all rotated 90 degrees clockwise.
Faint reversed red text visible through the paper from the other side, partially legible:

elements.description: Multiple administrative ink stamps and faint reverse-side text impressions on a paper back page. Visible stamps include: 'REC'D ESPIONAGE FBI' dated Nov 18 1:25 PM '57; 'RECEIVED-DIRECTOR FBI' dated Nov 15 11:09 AM '57; 'RECEIVED INTERNAL SECURITY SECT. FBI' dated Nov 8 11:22 AM '57; and 'REC'D - ESPIONAGE FBI' dated Nov 8 1:50 PM '57. Three black hole-punch marks are visible at the top edge.

judge.reason: Description A states there are two punched holes, while Description B states there are three, a direct contradiction on object count; moreover, Description B provides specific quoted stamp text that Description A does not see at all.
